metadata.title: Early Warning from Drift Before Direct Symmetry Detection
metadata.description: This technical note presents a four-benchmark empirical claim package for early warning from drift before direct symmetry detection. The note tests ordering in the gradual regime, reversal in an instant-break control, finite-budget sensitivity, and exact alarm-time separation. In the benchmarked paired-MLP regime, all four benchmarks support the claim package, showing that drift functions as an earlier and practically useful warning signal than direct symmetry detection under gradual symmetry breaking. A follow-on detector-latency sweep varies probe cadence, baseline count, and confirmation rules and finds that the latency-normalized regime split remains stable across the tested grid, strengthening the interpretation that the core result is not explained away by detector mechanics alone.
